examples.title: Truth Table Generator
examples.description: Generates truth table for any compound proposition
examples.code:
FUNCTION TruthTable(numVars):
 numRows = 2^numVars
 FOR i FROM 0 TO numRows-1:
 row = []
 FOR j FROM 0 TO numVars-1:
 // Calculate truth value using binary representation
 bit = (i // (2^j)) % 2
 row.append(bit == 1) // True if bit is 1
 PRINT row
 RETURN

// Example for 3 variables P, Q, R
// Output: 8 rows with all combinations
examples.explanation: Generates all 2^n combinations of truth values for n propositional variables by treating rows as binary numbers from 0 to 2^n-1.

examples.title: Evaluate Compound Proposition
examples.description: Evaluates a proposition given truth values for variables
examples.explanation: Recursively evaluates any compound proposition. For implication, implements the truth table: P→Q is false only when P is true and Q is false.

examples.title: Check Logical Equivalence
examples.description: Determines if two propositions are logically equivalent
examples.explanation: Tests both propositions against all possible truth value combinations. If results match for every combination, they are logically equivalent.
